MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.covers: Q1 FY27 10-Q (May 2026) + Q1 FY27 earnings 8-K + FY26 10-K + Q3 FY26 10-Q
NVDA.headline: DC growth re-accelerated to +73% YoY in Q1 FY27 with networking inside DC up ~199%; $124B customer purchase commitments; Q2 FY27 guided $45.5B; Vera Rubin on track Q3 FY27.

shifts.trend: Data Center YoY: Q3 FY26 +66% → FY26 full-year +68% → Q1 FY27 +73%; total revenue Q1 FY27 $44.1B (+69% YoY); Q2 FY27 guided $45.5B ±2% implies further QoQ growth
shifts.note: Growth reaccelerating on Blackwell ramp; Vera Rubin (next-gen) sampling Q3 FY27 extends runway

shifts.trend: Networking within Data Center: Q3 FY26 ~$8.2B (+162% YoY) → Q1 FY27 ~$14.8B (+~199% YoY); >90% networking attach rate on GB200/NVL rack-scale systems
shifts.note: Spectrum-X + InfiniBand dual-platform strategy; networking is now ~38% of Data Center revenue

shifts.trend: Hyperscalers ~50% of Q1 FY27 revenue; AI cloud (ACIE) ~50%; Q3 FY26 had four direct customers each >10% (22/15/13/11%); Q1 FY27 10-Q did not disclose individual >10% customers
shifts.note: $124B customer purchase commitments at quarter-end provides multi-quarter visibility despite concentration

shifts.trend: Supply constraints cited as power, land, and shell — NOT components; $124B purchase commitments from customers (demand far exceeds supply); NVDA investing $3.5B in land/power/shell guarantees to early AI customers
shifts.note: Revenue/GW improving from Hopper→Blackwell transition; data center, energy, and capital availability are the binding constraint on customer deployments

shifts.trend: H20 revenue effectively zero since Apr 2025 export controls; FY26 Q1 $4.5B inventory charge already taken; no meaningful China revenue contribution in Q1 FY27; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
shifts.note: China business structurally excluded; US export control regime stable at current levels through Q1 FY27

shifts.trend: Non-GAAP gross margin 73.5% Q1 FY27; guided 73.5% Q2 FY27; GAAP gross margin compresses modestly on Blackwell full-system mix; Hopper HGX→Blackwell transition completed
shifts.note: Margin durability confirmed: mix shift to Blackwell full systems no longer a headwind

NVDA.verdict: Demand remains supply-constrained — $124B in customer commitments makes the forward book unprecedented. DC growth re-accelerated in Q1 FY27 while networking (Spectrum-X/InfiniBand) grew ~199% YoY to ~$14.8B. Vera Rubin on track for Q3 FY27 sampling. China remains structurally zero. The only risk near-term is supply (power/land/shell), not demand. Maintain highest conviction.

AMAT.headline: Revenue dipped to -2% YoY in Q1 FY26 as trailing-edge logic weakness offset HBM-driven DRAM mix rotation; China pressure moderating.

shifts.trend: SemiSys revenue: $20.8B FY25 (+4% YoY) → $5.1B Q1 FY26 (-8% YoY); total revenue $28.4B +4% → $7.0B -2%
shifts.note: Trailing-edge logic softness offsets memory tailwind

shifts.trend: DRAM share of SemiSys mix: 26% (FY25) → 34% (Q1 FY26, +7pts) as HBM technology-transition spend accelerates; foundry/logic fell 67%→62%
shifts.note: Mix rotation toward higher-value memory beneficial long-term

shifts.trend: China revenue: $8.5B (-16% FY25) → $2.1B (-7% Q1 FY26); Taiwan surged +46% as leading-edge investment shifted
shifts.note: China pressure moderating from extreme -16% but still negative

shifts.trend: Gross margin 48.7% (FY25) → 49.0% (Q1 FY26); one-time $253M BIS legal settlement in Q1 depressed operating margin to 26.1% (-4.3pts)
shifts.note: Underlying margin trajectory positive; settlement is non-recurring

shifts.trend: Two BIS-compliance charges: $181M Q4 FY25 restructuring + $407M CAMT valuation allowance; $253M Q1 FY26 BIS settlement for prior China shipments
shifts.note: Prior China compliance exposure now settled — risk de-risking

AMAT.verdict: Thesis intact but in a soft patch — HBM/DRAM mix rotation is the growth vector, but trailing-edge weakness creates a revenue drag until leading-edge logic recovers. The BIS settlement removes regulatory overhang.

AMD.headline: Data Center growth re-accelerated from +32% to +57% YoY with gross margin expanding to 53%; China MI325 partial license with 25% tariff is partial recovery only.

shifts.note: MI350 + EPYC driving hyperscaler pull-through

shifts.trend: Gross margin: ~50% (FY25) → 53% (Q1 FY26, +3pts); DC operating margin 22%→28%
shifts.note: Richer mix shift toward GPU/DC products

shifts.trend: MI308 $800M charge Q2 FY25 → $360M partial reversal Q4 FY25 → MI325 licenses granted Feb 2026 with 25% US re-import tariff
shifts.note: Partial unlock only; tariff burden makes MI325 China uneconomic at scale

shifts.trend: International sales: 66% (FY25) → 74% (Q1 FY26); no single customer >10% in FY25; hyperscaler GPU locations driving geographic mix
shifts.note: Customer concentration not flagged — different risk profile from NVDA

shifts.trend: ZT Systems acquired $3.2B (Mar 2025); manufacturing arm sold $2.4B to Sanmina (Oct 2025); Helios rack-scale platform retained; R&D +39% to $2.4B Q1 FY26
shifts.note: Asset-light model on manufacturing, heavy R&D investment

AMD.verdict: Thesis strengthening — DC growth acceleration to +57% confirms AMD is taking real GPU wallet share; China is a limited partial unlock with tariff overhead; margins expanding, not compressing, as mix improves.

AMKR.headline: Revenue re-accelerated to +27.5% in Q1 with all four end markets growing; 2026 capex jumping to $2.5-3.0B (vs $0.9B in 2025) signals Arizona advanced-packaging bet.

shifts.trend: FY25 net sales $6.71B +6.2% → Q1 2026 $1.68B +27.5%; computing end market +16% FY25 → +19% Q1; comms +42%
shifts.note: All end markets accelerating simultaneously — broad-based demand

shifts.trend: FY25 capex $904.6M (13.5% of sales) → Q1 2026 $224.6M (annualized ~$900M pace, but FY26 guided $2.5-3.0B); Arizona facility construction beginning
shifts.note: Arizona advanced-packaging fab is a multi-year commitment with CHIPS Act support

shifts.trend: Gross margin 14.0% (FY25) → 14.2% (Q1 2026, +2.3pts YoY) on higher utilization
shifts.note: Arizona ramp-related inefficiencies may pressure margins in H2 2026

shifts.trend: Apple 29.8% of FY25 net sales, Qualcomm 11.1%; top-10 customers = 72% of 2025 sales — unchanged QoQ
shifts.note: Apple dependency persists — iPhone AI/PC upgrade cycle is the swing factor

AMKR.verdict: Thesis intact and investing through the cycle — the Arizona advanced-packaging bet is a long-term moat investment; near-term revenue acceleration supports the thesis while we wait for 2028 Arizona production.

AMZN.headline: AWS growth re-accelerated from +20% to +28% YoY; AWS RPO jumped to $364B on OpenAI's $100B expansion; Q1 capex $43.2B (+78% YoY) signals 2026 as the infrastructure peak year.

shifts.trend: AWS YoY: FY25 $128.7B +20% → Q1 2026 $37.6B +28%; AWS operating income: $45.6B (35.4% margin) → $14.16B (37.7% Q1)
shifts.note: Re-acceleration driven by AI workloads and Trainium adoption
2026 annualized pace ~$170B+ if Q1 run-rate holds

shifts.trend: AWS RPO: $244B (Dec 2025) → $364B (Mar 2026, +49% in one quarter) on $100B OpenAI expansion + existing $38B AWS deal; OpenAI now single largest cloud commitment
shifts.note: OpenAI concentration is both a risk and a demand signal

shifts.trend: $15B OpenAI Series C; committed $50B total to OpenAI; $5B Anthropic preferred + $20B Anthropic compute facility
shifts.note: Amazon is the hyperscaler most committed to AI frontier model investment

AMZN.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — AWS demand is re-accelerating and the OpenAI/Anthropic investment portfolio creates durable cloud demand. Capex surge is both a near-term FCF headwind and a multi-year revenue setup.

ANET.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerated to +35% with product deferred revenue surging +$826M in one quarter; Cloud/AI Titan concentration rose further as AI Ethernet wins compound.

shifts.note: AI Ethernet deployments pulling revenue above trend

shifts.trend: Product deferred revenue: $5.37B (FY25) → $6.20B (Q1 FY26, +$826M in 90 days); $1.2B other performance obligations added
shifts.note: Backlog build implies demand materially outstripping delivery capacity

shifts.trend: Gross margin: 64.1% (FY25) → 61.9% (Q1 FY26, -2.2pts) on large-customer mix shift
shifts.note: Hyperscaler-mix margin compression; partially transitory per historical pattern

shifts.trend: Cloud and AI Titans: 48% (FY25) → implied rising Q1 as AI Ethernet orders dominate backlog
shifts.note: Hyperscaler concentration is core to the thesis, not a new risk

ANET.verdict: Thesis intact and building — the deferred revenue surge (+$826M in one quarter) is the clearest forward indicator; GM compression is a hyperscaler-mix feature, not a competitive pricing issue.

AVGO.headline: Semiconductor solutions re-accelerated to +52% YoY on custom AI XPUs and AI networking; top-5 customer concentration tightened to ~50% of quarterly revenue.

shifts.trend: Semiconductor solutions: FY25 $36.9B +22% → Q1 FY26 $12.5B +52%; total revenue FY25 $63.9B +24% → Q1 $19.3B +29%
shifts.note: Custom AI XPU ramp pulling semiconductor segment well above trend

shifts.trend: Top-5 end customers: ~40%+ of FY25 revenue → ~50% of Q1 FY26 revenue
shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependency deepening as custom silicon becomes majority of semi revenue

shifts.trend: Infrastructure software: $27.0B FY25 +26% → $6.8B Q1 FY26 +1%; VMware integration synergies largely captured
shifts.note: Software slowdown expected; semi more than compensates on AI ramp

shifts.trend: Broad mix: software (high margin) slowing, semi (improving mix) accelerating; overall margins holding as custom silicon economics improve
shifts.note: Custom XPU economics better than commodity GPU — moat thesis confirmed

AVGO.verdict: Thesis intact — custom AI XPU franchise is compounding; the software slowdown is noise against a +52% semiconductor segment. Top-5 at 50% of revenue is the key concentration risk to monitor.

CDNS.headline: Revenue growth accelerated to +19% with China +36% YoY despite BIS overhang; backlog grew to $8.0B — EDA spend outpacing WFE.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $5.30B +14% → Q1 FY26 $1.47B +19%; product & maintenance +14% FY25 → +21% Q1
shifts.note: AI chip design complexity driving EDA revenue above prior trend

shifts.trend: China revenue: $680M +19% (FY25, 13% of total) → $189M +36% (Q1 FY26, 13% of Q1); BIS 'is-informed' rescinded Jul 2025
shifts.note: China growth re-accelerated after BIS risk removed; affiliate rules still a watch item

shifts.trend: RPO backlog: $7.8B (FY25, 53% in next 12mo) → $8.0B (Q1 FY26, 55% in next 12mo); cRPO +$200M
shifts.note: Backlog growth signals multi-quarter demand visibility

shifts.trend: BIS 'is-informed' EDA license imposed May 23 2025 → rescinded July 2, 2025; Sep 2025 BIS 50%-owned-affiliate rule flagged as ongoing watch
shifts.note: Export-control risk reduced but not eliminated

CDNS.verdict: Thesis intact — EDA is growing faster than hardware (WFE), a structural advantage as chip complexity compounds. China upside is real but subject to recurring BIS whiplash.

CEG.headline: Calpine acquisition closed Jan 7, creating the world's largest private power producer at 55 GW; nuclear PPAs with hyperscalers continue stacking as the clean baseload premium expands.

shifts.trend: Pre-Calpine: ~32 GW nuclear + small gas → Post-Calpine: 55 GW total (21 GW gas + 33 GW nuclear including Three Mile Island/Crane restart)
shifts.note: Calpine diversifies into gas but nuclear fleet remains the margin premium

shifts.trend: Meta PPA (Clinton, 20-year) signed Q3 2025; Calpine adds Microsoft and other tech hyperscaler PPA relationships; Microsoft Three Mile Island (Crane) 20-year agreement in progress
shifts.note: Hyperscaler PPA book building — multi-decade clean power commitments

shifts.trend: Calpine consideration ~$22B (50M shares + $4.5B cash); additional Crane restart capex; Calpine PPAs fund incremental gas capacity investments
shifts.note: Balance sheet significantly levered post-Calpine — debt reduction is the near-term priority

shifts.trend: Calpine acquisition added ~$4.5B cash consideration + assumed Calpine debt; combined company leverage now materially higher pre-acquisition
shifts.note: PPA cash flows secure deleveraging path but execution risk elevated

CEG.verdict: Thesis expanding in scope — Calpine makes CEG the dominant US power company for hyperscaler nuclear + gas baseload needs. The thesis shifts from pure-nuclear to nuclear + gas; leverage is the key near-term risk.

CLS.headline: Revenue +53% YoY in Q1 with HPS networking switch programs driving 42% of total; revolver upsized from $750M to $1.75B signals continued capacity scale-up.

shifts.note: HBM rate decelerating vs FY25 (+81%) but on much larger base; CCS re-accelerated

shifts.trend: 3 CCS customers >10% (32%/14%/12%) FY25; Enterprise +101% Q1 (next-gen AI/ML ramp); Communications +69%
shifts.note: Enterprise re-inflected on next-gen AI compute after FY25 pause — positive surprise

shifts.trend: HPS (hyperscaler networking switches): 29% of FY24 → 41% FY25 → 42% Q1 FY26; CCS segment margin: 8.4% Q4 FY25 → 8.6% Q1 FY26
shifts.note: HPS share and margin both expanding — operational leverage kicking in

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guided ~$1B (~6% of revenue) vs historical 1.5-2.0%; revolver upsized $750M→$1.75B; expansions in Thailand/US/Mexico/Japan for AI capacity
shifts.note: Significant capacity addition in progress across multiple geographies

CLS.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — CLS is the clearest non-fabless beneficiary of hyperscaler AI networking capex; the revolver upsizing confirms management visibility into H2 2026 demand. Customer concentration in top-3 is the key risk.

COHR.headline: D&C segment growth accelerated from +34% to +41% with 'scale across' AI interconnect emerging as a new demand category; gross margin expanded 243 bps YoY to 38%.

shifts.trend: D&C segment: Q2 FY26 $1.21B +34% → Q3 FY26 $1.36B +41%; total revenue +17% → +21%
shifts.note: Acceleration is demand-driven, not mix; 'scale across' interconnect adds a new growth vector

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q2 37% (+140bps YoY) → Q3 38% (+243bps YoY); 9-month consolidated GM 37%
shifts.note: D&C yield improvements and pricing optimization are durable margin drivers

shifts.trend: Industrial: Q2 -10% (aerospace divestiture) → Q3 -16% (Munich + aerospace divestitures); portfolio narrowing to AI/data-center optics
shifts.note: Industrial drag is deliberate — management high-grading the portfolio

shifts.trend: Interest expense -$13M Q3 (-$39M YTD) on term-loan paydown; $14M Q3 equity-investment gain; balance sheet improving as divestitures complete
shifts.note: Portfolio rationalization generating cash for debt paydown

COHR.verdict: Thesis strengthening — AI datacenter transceiver demand is re-accelerating and COHR now has 'scale across' inter-datacenter AI traffic as a new demand source. Portfolio cleanup reduces Industrial distraction.

CRM.headline: RPO inflected from QoQ dip to $72.4B full-year (+14%); cRPO +16% signals near-term acceleration; Agentforce driving the only double-digit segment growth.

shifts.trend: Revenue: Q3 FY26 $10.26B +9% → FY26 $41.5B +10%; Agentforce 360 Platform +19% is the sole double-digit segment
shifts.note: Overall growth stable but AI products creating a divergence within segments

shifts.trend: RPO: Q3 FY26 $59.5B (dipped QoQ from $63.4B) → FY26 year-end $72.4B +14%; cRPO $35.1B +16%
shifts.note: cRPO outpacing total RPO — favorable signal for near-term revenue recognition

shifts.trend: Operating margin: Q3 FY26 ~19% → FY26 20% (expanded 1pt YoY); diluted EPS $7.80 vs $6.36
shifts.note: Discipline maintained even as Informatica integration begins

shifts.trend: Informatica acquisition $9.6B closed; integrating Informatica's data-intelligence platform to power AI Data Cloud + Agentforce
shifts.note: Informatica integration is a 12-18 month integration risk and opportunity

CRM.verdict: Thesis intact but requires Agentforce to inflect — current +9-10% revenue growth is below peers; the RPO rebuild and cRPO +16% suggest acceleration ahead, but Informatica integration adds execution complexity.

CRWV.headline: Microsoft concentration fell from 67% to 45% as Meta emerged at 20%; RPO surged to $98.8B (+63% in one quarter); net loss widening as capacity investments ramp.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $5.1B +168% → Q1 2026 $2.08B +112% (base-effect deceleration on much larger base)
shifts.note: Growth still triple-digit — decel is arithmetic, not demand

shifts.trend: Microsoft: 67% FY25 → 45% Q1 2026; Meta emerged at 20%; OpenAI $11.9B contract through Oct 2027
shifts.note: Diversification is rapid and intentional — key de-risking signal

shifts.trend: RPO: $60.7B (FY25) → $98.8B (Q1 2026, +63% in 90 days); 36% recognized next 24mo, 39% months 25-48
shifts.note: RPO growth implies 2026-2028 revenue already partially locked

shifts.trend: Net loss: FY25 $1.2B → Q1 2026 $740M (annualized ~$3B); accumulated deficit $3.4B; $850M active, 3.1 GW contracted power
shifts.note: Cash burn is front-loaded on capacity build; RPO coverage is the key solvency check

CRWV.verdict: Thesis intact for neocloud infrastructure play — customer diversification is rapid and impressive; RPO $98.8B at Q1 implies 2+ years of revenue visibility; net loss widening is expected during the ramp, but monitoring the cash/RPO coverage ratio is essential.

CSCO.headline: Networking product growth accelerated from +15% to +21% in Q2 on AI Infrastructure; product gross margin and EPS improving as Silicon One capacity investments scale.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: Q1 $14.88B +8% → Q2 $15.35B +10%; Networking: Q1 +15% → Q2 +21%; 1H FY26 Networking +18%
shifts.note: AI Infrastructure + Data Center Switching + Campus driving multi-product acceleration

shifts.trend: Inventory +7% and purchase commitments +10% vs FYE 2025 in Q1; Q2 continued Silicon One capacity buildup for hyperscalers
shifts.note: Supply chain build signals management confidence in H2 FY26 demand

shifts.trend: Security: Q2 -4% on Splunk shift from on-prem to cloud subscriptions; trend expected to continue 2H FY26
shifts.note: Security drag is structural (cloud transition), not demand-driven

shifts.trend: Product GM: Q2 +0.2pts on productivity offsetting mix; diluted EPS +31%; effective tax rate 12.9% vs 15.9% prior year
shifts.note: Tax rate improvement and productivity adding to EPS leverage

CSCO.verdict: Thesis intact — AI Infrastructure is the clear growth engine and Networking is re-accelerating above trend. The Security -4% headwind from Splunk transition is the one metric to watch as it creates a drag on overall growth.

ETN.headline: Electrical Americas backlog +44% YoY to $14.5B with organic orders +42% in Q1 — data center power buildout driving sustained multi-year revenue visibility.

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas backlog: $13.2B (FY25, +31% YoY) → $14.5B (Q1 FY26, +44% YoY); book-to-bill 1.2x both periods
shifts.note: Backlog growth accelerating — customers committing further out

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas sales: FY25 $13.3B +16% → Q1 FY26 $3.60B +20%; Electrical Global Q1 +21%; total consolidated Q1 +25%
shifts.note: Broad-based acceleration across geographies with data center as common driver

shifts.trend: Boyd Thermal (data center cooling) acquisition $9.5B announced; Eaton now extending from power distribution into thermal management for data centers
shifts.note: Boyd Thermal expands TAM into cooling — direct comp with VRT/NVT in liquid cooling

shifts.trend: Electrical Americas operating margin: ~20%+ both periods; Q1 total operating income +52% on volume leverage
shifts.note: Incremental margins expanding as revenue scales through fixed-cost base

ETN.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — Eaton is one of the clearest beneficiaries of the data-center power buildout; the Boyd Thermal deal extends the moat into cooling. Backlog +44% is the most bullish forward indicator in this cohort.

FN.headline: DCI module revenue +90.4% YoY as 1.6T hyperscaler interconnect ramp surges; total revenue re-accelerated to +39.3% from +35.9%.

shifts.note: Steady acceleration driven by DCI surge outpacing comms and auto

shifts.trend: DCI modules: Q2 mid-double-digit growth → Q3 $196.9M +90.4%; Telecom $412.2M → $499.7M +42.5%; Datacom +11.3%
shifts.note: DCI becoming the lead growth driver as 1.6T coherent interconnect deployments ramp

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q2 12.2% → Q3 11.9% (-20bps QoQ); operating income $120M (9.9% margin) +52.1% YoY
shifts.note: GM compression on DCI mix (premium product but lower gross-margin percentage); operating leverage compensates

shifts.trend: Fabrinet has historically served Cisco, Lumentum, Coherent as top customers; no new disclosure of customer shift in these quarters
shifts.note: Stable customer mix confirms broad-based optical demand

FN.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — the DCI +90% print confirms that 1.6T hyperscaler interconnect is a real supercycle; Fabrinet's EMS model provides high-confidence manufacturing leverage without customer risk.

GEV.headline: RPO surged to $163.3B (+32% YoY) with equipment RPO jumping to $75.9B; HA-Turbine deliveries driving Gas Power EBITDA +57% in Q1.

shifts.trend: Total RPO: $150.2B (FY25) → $163.3B (Q1 2026, +9% QoQ, +32% YoY); Equipment RPO: $64.2B → $75.9B (+18% in one quarter)
shifts.note: Multi-year equipment delivery queue locked in — pricing power compounding

shifts.trend: Heavy-Duty Gas Turbine orders: 41 (2023) → 68 (2024) → 110 (2025) → Q1 2026: 12 units already (HA-class), run-rate +100% YoY
shifts.note: Turbine order velocity unprecedented in modern grid history

shifts.trend: Gas Power EBITDA: FY25 strong absolute → Q1 2026 +57% YoY; equipment deliveries at premium margin on HA-class units
shifts.note: HA-turbines (most efficient) carry the highest margin in the fleet

shifts.trend: GEV maintains asset-light services model (services RPO $68.4B → $87.4B) alongside equipment; services provide annuity-like revenue on installed base
shifts.note: Services RPO +27% YoY provides earnings floor independent of new orders

GEV.verdict: Thesis intact and commanding — RPO at $163.3B with equipment alone at $75.9B implies 3-4 years of locked-in deliveries. Turbine order velocity is the clearest physical constraint in the inference-stack power layer.

GNRC.headline: C&I segment turned +28% in Q1 on data center generator demand after a soft FY25; overall revenue recovered to +12% with residential drag easing.

shifts.note: Data center demand is the inflection point for C&I; residential recovery adds to the momentum

shifts.trend: C&I $510M +28% in Q1 (data center + industrial); residential recovering as outage season normalizes from FY25 quiet period
shifts.note: FY25 quiet outage year created a tough comp that's now lapping

shifts.trend: Net income: FY25 $159.6M (4% of sales) → Q1 FY26 $73M ($1.24 EPS vs $0.73 prior year, +70% EPS improvement)
shifts.note: Operating leverage on higher volumes driving significant EPS recovery

GNRC.verdict: Thesis intact — data center generator demand is the secular growth driver for GNRC's C&I segment; residential recovery adds a cyclical tailwind. The combination should sustain double-digit revenue growth through 2026.

GOOGL.headline: Google Cloud growth nearly doubled to +63% YoY in Q1; Q1 capex $35.7B vs $91.4B full-year 2025 implies ~$143B annual run-rate for 2026 — significant step-up.

shifts.trend: Google Cloud: FY25 $58.7B +36% → Q1 2026 $20.0B +63%; Cloud op income $13.9B FY25 (24% margin) → $6.6B Q1 (33% margin, 3x YoY)
shifts.note: Cloud op income tripling YoY — AI pull-through now at inflection

shifts.trend: Capex: FY24 $52.5B → FY25 $91.4B (+74%) → Q1 2026 alone $35.7B (annualized ~$143B, +56% vs FY25)
shifts.note: Mgmt committed to 'significantly increase' capex in 2026; Q1 confirms pace

shifts.trend: Revenue backlog: FY25 $242.8B → Q1 2026 $467.6B (+93% in one quarter); GCP contractual backlog nearly doubled
shifts.note: Backlog surge of $224.8B in 90 days — largest hyperscaler demand signal I/O 2026

shifts.trend: Cloud operating margin: 24% (FY25) → 33% (Q1 2026); total GAAP operating margin 32% (Q1 2026)
shifts.note: Scale economics compounding as cloud fixed costs dilute over growing revenue base

GOOGL.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — Google Cloud's +63% print and margin expansion to 33% confirm AI demand is real and Alphabet is winning share. The capex surge to $143B annualized is both bullish (demand) and the primary FCF headwind.

HPE.headline: Networking +151.5% on full-quarter Juniper inclusion masks underlying Cloud & AI server decline of -2.7%; Juniper synergy realization is the 2026 execution test.

shifts.trend: Total revenue: FY25 $34.3B +13.8% → Q1 FY26 $9.3B +18.4%; Networking: FY25 +51.1% → Q1 FY26 +151.5% (full Juniper quarter)
shifts.note: Networking acceleration is entirely Juniper comparables — not organic AI networking

shifts.trend: Cloud & AI (new segment = Server + Hybrid Cloud + Financial Services): Q1 FY26 -2.7% on server AUP headwinds; prior comparable excluded Juniper from networking
shifts.note: Server weakness is the real signal — HPE losing ground in AI server vs pure-play ODMs

shifts.trend: Gross margin held as Juniper services (high margin) offset server pressure; FY25 GAAP operating loss $437M on $1.6B Hybrid Cloud goodwill impairment (non-cash)
shifts.note: The goodwill impairment signals Hybrid Cloud repositioning — not a recurring charge

shifts.trend: Networking now ~29% of Q1 revenue (vs ~14% pre-Juniper); segment concentration in Juniper-driven networking creates dependency
shifts.note: Juniper integration synergies must offset server weakness by Q3-Q4 FY26

HPE.verdict: Story clouded — Juniper's full-quarter inclusion inflates headline growth; the Cloud & AI server decline is the concerning underlying signal. Thesis depends on whether Juniper networking synergies can offset server AUP compression.

HUBB.headline: Revenue accelerated from +3.8% to +11% YoY with Electrical Solutions organic +10.6%; data center demand sustained as backlog grows to $2.16B+.

shifts.trend: Total sales: FY25 $5.84B +3.8% organic → Q1 FY26 $1.52B +11% (+8.2% organic); Electrical Solutions: +7.1% FY25 → +10.6% organic Q1
shifts.note: Organic acceleration across both segments — pricing and volume both contributing

shifts.trend: Total backlog: FY25 $2.16B (+14% YoY) → Q1 FY26 growing further; substantially all to ship in 2026
shifts.note: Short-cycle backlog (12 months) provides 2026 revenue visibility

shifts.trend: FY25 adjusted operating margin 22.5% (+80bps); Q1 FY26 operating income +% on volume leverage; pricing/productivity offset tariff
shifts.note: Tariff headwinds managed via price; not impacting margin trajectory

shifts.trend: Electrical Solutions (datacenter/industrial): FY25 +7.1% organic → Q1 FY26 +10.6% organic; Utility Solutions: FY25 +1.1% organic → Q1 FY26 +6.8%
shifts.note: Both segments accelerating — datacenter + utility grid investment double-barreled

HUBB.verdict: Thesis intact — Hubbell is a steady beneficiary of both data-center electrification and utility grid hardening. The acceleration from +4% to +11% confirms both end-markets are durable.

INTC.headline: Products re-growing with DCAI +22% on premium mix; supply constraints emerged as a new bottleneck through H1 2026; Intel Foundry loss widened and 14A path uncertain.

shifts.note: DCAI premium-mix pricing driving ASP +27% even as volume -5% due to supply constraints

shifts.trend: 'Market demand exceeded our available product supply in Q1 2026...limiting our ability to fully meet customer demand. Expect constraints to persist at least through H1 2026.' — new language vs FY25 10-K
shifts.note: Supply constraint is a new bottleneck signal — unusual for Intel (historically supply-push)

shifts.trend: Intel Foundry: FY25 $10.3B operating loss ($307M external revenue) → Q1 2026 $2.4B loss ($174M external revenue); Altera now external after Sep 2025 deconsolidation
shifts.note: Foundry external revenue actually falling — no meaningful external customer secured

shifts.trend: CHIPS Act: $6.4B direct funding committed; 35% ITC on qualified property; SoftBank $2.0B + NVIDIA $5.0B equity investments; Aug 2025 159M USG shares escrow for CHIPS Secure Enclave
shifts.note: CHIPS support de-risks capex but $10B/year Foundry losses remain the math problem

INTC.verdict: Story bifurcated — Products supply-constrained recovery is real but Foundry remains a structural drag with no credible path to external revenue. The 14A pause risk is the binary option on whether Intel becomes a leading-edge foundry or outsources to TSMC.

KLAC.headline: Record quarterly revenue $3.42B (+11% YoY) driven by Korea +80% on HBM/DRAM investment; China share contracted to 24% as control market rotated to memory.

shifts.trend: Revenue: Q2 FY26 $3.30B +7% → Q3 FY26 $3.42B +11%; SPC segment: +9% → +13%
shifts.note: Steady acceleration driven by memory investment ramp

shifts.trend: Korea: Q2 +34% to $505M → Q3 +80% to $681M; Taiwan: Q2 leading (not broken out) → Q3 $869M (-12% on timing); North America: +38% → +40%
shifts.note: Korea surge confirms SK Hynix HBM and Samsung DRAM investment is pulling KLAC

shifts.trend: China: Q2 $995M (30.2% of revenue, -9% YoY) → Q3 $830M (24.3%, +5% YoY modest); year-to-date China $3.09B flat
shifts.note: China share declining as Taiwan/Korea grow faster — risk profile improving

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q2 61.4% (+1.1pts YoY) → Q3 61.1% (-0.5pts QoQ); DRAM chip costs in image computers flagged as transitory headwind
shifts.note: DRAM input cost for KLAC's own products is ironic — flagged as transitory

KLAC.verdict: Thesis intact — KLAC's process-control equipment is the tightest bottleneck in the memory supply chain; Korea +80% on HBM investment confirms the demand signal. China contraction reduces long-term risk.

LEU.headline: HALEU production moving to execution with Fluor EPC contract signed; backlog at $3.9B with $2.4B contingent on US government demand decisions.

shifts.trend: Backlog: $3.8B (FY25) → $3.9B (Q1 FY26); $900M HALEU task order (Jan 5, 2026); contingent backlog $2.3B→$2.4B
shifts.note: Backlog growing but contingent portion means demand isn't fully locked

shifts.trend: Fluor EPC contract signed Feb 2026 for Piketon LEU+HALEU expansion; HALEU production task order commenced for commercial-scale output
shifts.note: Execution phase begins — capex will ramp sharply in 2026-2028

shifts.trend: Q1 FY26 revenue $76.7M +5%; LEU revenue $44.6M -13% (SWU volume -47%, price +52%); Technical Solutions $32.1M +47% on HALEU contract
shifts.note: SWU revenue volatile on shipment timing; Technical Solutions is the growth engine now

LEU.verdict: Thesis intact but pre-commercial-scale — LEU is executing on the government-funded pathway to HALEU production; revenue is small and volatile, payoff is 3-5 years; the Fluor EPC contract is a genuine milestone.

LITE.headline: Revenue growth accelerated to +90% YoY in Q3 with gross margin expanding 1,540 bps to 44.2% on the 200G laser chip and cloud transceiver ramp — the clearest optical inflection in the cohort.

shifts.note: Sequential acceleration despite large base — demand is not slowing
Step-function margin expansion as 200G laser chips hit volume yield; structural not transitory
Systems acceleration (+121%) suggests hyperscaler transceiver orders pulling ahead of plan

shifts.trend: Strong operational leverage — GAAP operating margin recovered from -12.8% prior year Q2 to 9.7% Q2 → positive Q3; balance sheet improving
shifts.note: Turnaround from operating loss to profitability in two quarters confirms leverage point

LITE.verdict: Thesis strongly intact and accelerating — LITE is at the inflection point of the 200G cycle; margin expansion of 1,540 bps YoY is structural. This is one of the clearest bottleneck-expansion plays in the optical layer.

LRCX.headline: Revenue at $5.84B (+9% QoQ) with memory mix rising to 39% on DRAM surge; Korea growing as HBM investment drives the DRAM cycle; China stable at 34% of revenue.

shifts.trend: Revenue: Q2 $5.34B (flat QoQ); Q3 $5.84B (+9% QoQ); 9M $16.51B vs $13.26B prior year (+24%)
shifts.note: QoQ re-acceleration in Q3 on DRAM investment surge

shifts.trend: Memory mix: Q2 34% → Q3 39% (+5pts QoQ); DRAM leading with NVM recovering; Foundry: Q2 59% → Q3 54%
shifts.note: DRAM pulling share from foundry as HBM investment ramps at SK Hynix/Samsung

shifts.trend: China: Q2 35% → Q3 34% of quarterly revenue; constrained by US export controls but stable
shifts.note: China stable at 34% — risk plateau rather than further deterioration

shifts.trend: Gross margin: Q2 49.6% → Q3 49.8% (+0.2pts QoQ); net income $1.83B (+15% QoQ) on operating leverage
shifts.note: GM holding despite mix shifts — systems efficiency improving

LRCX.verdict: Thesis intact — LRCX benefits from both the HBM-driven DRAM investment cycle and the recoveries in NVM; China plateau rather than deterioration reduces incremental risk. Memory inflection is the key upside catalyst.

META.headline: Revenue accelerated to +33% YoY in Q1; 2026 capex guidance raised $10B at midpoint to $125-145B — META is the largest incremental buyer of AI compute infrastructure.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $201.0B +22% → Q1 2026 $56.3B +33%; ad impressions +19%, price per ad +12% Q1
shifts.note: AI recommendation systems driving simultaneous volume AND pricing improvement

shifts.trend: Capex: FY25 $72.2B → FY26 guided $115-135B (initial) → raised to $125-145B; Q1 2026 $19.8B (annualized ~$79B vs $72B FY25)
shifts.note: The $10B raise signals AI compute appetite exceeds initial budget plans

shifts.trend: Reality Labs operating loss: FY25 ~$17B → Q1 2026 $4.03B (run-rate ~$16B); mgmt expects FY26 RL losses to increase
shifts.note: RL is a portfolio drag; core social AI profitability more than offsets

shifts.trend: Non-cancelable commitments: $237.7B at Mar 2026 (up sharply); supply constraints across GPU, data center, and power cited as 2026 deployment gating factors
shifts.note: META is both a demand source and a bottleneck victim — competing with MSFT/GOOGL for AI compute

META.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — META is simultaneously the best proxy for AI-driven ad revenue inflection and the largest single demand source for inference infrastructure. The capex raise is the most directly bullish data point for the entire supply chain.

MOD.headline: Climate Solutions accelerated from +24% to +51% on data center growth; Reverse Morris Trust announced to create a pure-play data center cooling company.

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions: Q2 FY26 $454.4M +24% → Q3 FY26 $544.6M +51%; 9M data center product sales +$221.7M YoY
shifts.note: Data center cooling demand accelerating faster than capacity ramp-up

shifts.trend: Climate Solutions operating margin: Q2 13.7% (-440bps from rapid capacity expansion) → Q3 15.3% (still -200bps from temporary inefficiencies)
shifts.note: Margin recovering as capacity utilization improves but not yet back to ~17%+ levels

shifts.trend: Performance Technologies: Q2 -4% → Q3 part of discontinued operations pipeline; RMT announced Jan 2026 to spin/merge with Gentherm
shifts.note: PT spin is catalyst to unlock Climate Solutions multiple re-rating

shifts.trend: RMT valued at ~$1B; MOD shareholders get ~40% of Gentherm; MOD receives $210M cash at close (end CY2026); pure-play climate/data-center company emerging
shifts.note: Transaction value realization is CY26 close-dependent

MOD.verdict: Thesis intact and restructuring to focus — the RMT removes the PT distraction and should allow Climate Solutions' 50%+ growth to re-rate; margin recovery in Q3 vs Q2 is a positive trajectory.

MRVL.headline: FY26 Data Center revenue +46% to $6.1B (74% of total mix) on custom AI ASICs and electro-optics; full-year confirms the sustained trajectory seen in Q3.

shifts.trend: DC end-market: Q3 FY26 $1.52B (73%, +38% YoY) → FY26 $6.1B (74%, +46% YoY); total revenue FY26 $8.2B +42%
shifts.note: Annual growth accelerated from Q3's +38% to +46% on FY basis — ramp intensifying

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 41.3% → FY26 51.0% (+9.7pts); custom silicon economics vs commodity GPU clear in the gross-margin gap
shifts.note: GM expansion confirms custom ASIC moat — richer economics than merchant silicon

shifts.trend: Communications & other end-market $2.1B +31% (inventory correction recovery); Carrier infrastructure recovering after multi-quarter digestion
shifts.note: Carrier recovery adds a second growth vector beyond DC custom silicon

shifts.trend: FY26 10-K: expects US export restrictions on certain Chinese customers to continue impacting; flagged as ongoing headwind
shifts.note: China custom silicon restrictions are incremental to semiconductor equipment controls

MRVL.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — MRVL's custom AI ASIC franchise with hyperscalers is compounding at +46% with 51% gross margins. Carrier recovery adds defensiveness; China restriction is known and manageable at current scale.

MSFT.headline: Azure growth re-accelerated from +39% to +40% in Q3; Microsoft Cloud +29% YoY as AI services consumption inflects; commercial RPO holds at $627B.

shifts.trend: Azure: Q2 +39% → Q3 +40%; Microsoft Cloud: Q2 $51.5B +26% → Q3 $54.5B +29%
shifts.note: Re-acceleration signals AI services turning into consumption revenue

shifts.trend: Commercial RPO: Q2 $625B (+110% YoY) → Q3 $627B (+99% YoY); ~25% to recognize next 12mo
shifts.note: RPO flat QoQ but at a very high base — future revenue is deeply contracted

shifts.trend: H1 FY26 capex $49.3B; Q3 additions to property/equipment $30.9B; annualized FY26 pace well above $100B
shifts.note: Microsoft is the largest absolute capex spender globally in calendar 2026

shifts.trend: Intelligent Cloud (includes Azure): Q2 $31.9B → Q3 $32.9B; Productivity & Business: stable; Personal Computing: recovering
shifts.note: Intelligent Cloud growth outpacing all other segments as AI tilts mix

MSFT.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — Azure +40% with commercial RPO at $627B is the combination of current demand and forward contracts that makes MSFT the highest-confidence hyperscaler investment in this cohort.

MXL.headline: Revenue +43% YoY in Q1 led by Infrastructure (optical DSPs, wireless backhaul); recovery broadening from broadband into infrastructure as data center demand pulls.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $467.6M +30% → Q1 2026 $137.2M +43%; Infrastructure: FY25 +$34.3M → Q1 +$36.3M QoQ (now the lead segment)
shifts.note: Infrastructure surpassing broadband as the primary growth driver

shifts.trend: Broadband: FY25 44% of revenue (+59%) → Q1 2026 also growing but slower; Infrastructure (optical DSPs + wireless): accelerating as lead driver
shifts.note: Mix shift toward higher-ASP infrastructure products — margin-positive

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 57% (+3pts on mix shift); Q1 2026 carrying same trajectory as infrastructure mix increases
shifts.note: Optical DSP products have premium margins vs commodity broadband chips

MXL.verdict: Thesis intact — MXL's optical DSP and infrastructure connectivity are direct beneficiaries of the 400G/800G data center interconnect expansion. Small-cap volatility risk but product positioning is correct.

NOW.headline: Consistent 22% revenue growth maintained with RPO +25% at $27.7B; Now Assist AI deployed across all major product lines but not yet inflecting headline growth.

shifts.note: Extremely consistent growth rate — no AI inflection yet in headline numbers

shifts.trend: Total RPO: FY25 $28.2B +27% → Q1 2026 $27.7B +25%; cRPO 46% of RPO both periods (+25% and +23% respectively)
shifts.note: RPO growth stable — consistent demand pipeline without acceleration

shifts.trend: Now Assist deployed across ITSM, HRSD, CSM, Field Service, IRM, SecOps; agentic-AI use cases announced in FY26 10-K for all platform areas
shifts.note: AI features embedded but pricing/attach not yet visible in financials

NOW.verdict: Thesis intact but awaiting inflection — ServiceNow's AI platform investments are real and deeply embedded, but the +22% growth rate hasn't yet inflected with AI premium pricing. RPO stability is reassuring; the next 2 quarters will tell the AI monetization story.

NVT.headline: Organic revenue +34.4% in Q1 with Systems Protection +50.1% organic — data center thermal management demand inflecting sharply above FY25's already-strong 13% organic base.

shifts.trend: Total: FY25 $3.89B +30% (organic +13%) → Q1 FY26 $1.24B +53.5% (organic +34.4%); Systems Protection: FY25 +42.2% total → Q1 +76.1% total / +50.1% organic
shifts.note: Organic acceleration from 13% to 34% is dramatic — acquisition-adjusted signal

shifts.trend: Systems Protection $894.8M +76.1% Q1 (data center enclosures, thermal, UPS-adjacent); Electrical Connections $347.2M +15.3%
shifts.note: Systems Protection segment now 72% of Q1 revenue and accelerating — data center is the engine

shifts.trend: Systems Protection segment income +95% Q1; margin expanded +220bps; operating leverage from volume scale
shifts.note: Margin expansion confirms pricing power in constrained thermal management market

shifts.trend: FY25 acquisition activity driving +16.3pts of inorganic growth; continuing to bolt-on data center adjacencies
shifts.note: Acquisition strategy explicitly targeting data center thermal and power management

NVT.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — NVT's organic acceleration to +34% confirms data center demand is pulling through the entire thermal/power management category. Margin expansion adds conviction this is not just a pass-through revenue story.

OKLO.headline: Meta 1.2 GW Pike County prepayment agreement signed — the first hyperscaler cash prepayment for advanced fission; order book continues to compound from a low base.

shifts.trend: Order book: ~15,600 MWe (Q3 2025) → 18,100 MWe (latest); Meta 1.2 GW prepayment for Pike County, Ohio
shifts.note: Hyperscaler demand creating first genuine commercial validation for advanced fission

shifts.trend: Cash/securities: $1.18B (Q3 2025); FY25 target operating expenses $80-100M; well-funded through NRC licensing process
shifts.note: Aurora reactor NRC licensing expected to complete by 2025-2026; cash runway through first commercial plant

shifts.trend: FY25 opex $139.3M; R&D + G&A expanding as NRC application advances; Meta prepayment = first revenue event structure
shifts.note: Pre-revenue company — order book and licensing milestones are the metrics, not quarterly P&L

OKLO.verdict: Thesis intact as an option on advanced fission — the Meta prepayment is a transformational commercial milestone. Execution risk is high (NRC licensing, Aurora deployment), but the hyperscaler endorsement significantly de-risks demand.

ORCL.headline: OCI growth accelerated from +68% to +84% YoY; RPO surged to $552.6B vs $130.2B prior year, requiring $39.2B capex in 9 months to build out — Oracle is emerging as an AI cloud challenger.

shifts.note: OCI re-accelerating while MSFT/GOOGL are in the 30-40% range — catch-up dynamic

shifts.trend: RPO: Q2 $523.3B → Q3 $552.6B (vs $130.2B Q3 prior year, +324% YoY); 9M capex $39.2B vs ~$12B prior year
shifts.note: RPO $552.6B is larger than MSFT's $627B and GOOGL's $467B — concentrated AI workload demand

shifts.trend: 9M FY26 capex $39.2B (3x prior year pace); $42.7B senior notes issued; Oracle is levering up to fund data center build-out
shifts.note: Most aggressive balance-sheet expansion of any hyperscaler — RPO justifies the debt

shifts.trend: $42.7B senior notes issued in FY26; total debt substantially increased; FCF insufficient to self-fund at current capex pace
shifts.note: Debt-funded AI growth — sustainable if OCI contracts convert to revenue as expected

ORCL.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — OCI's +84% growth and $552.6B RPO (largest in this cohort relative to base) are the most dramatic demand signals in enterprise cloud. The debt burden is the risk but RPO coverage is deep.

PLTR.headline: Revenue growth re-accelerated to +85% YoY in Q1 with commercial +95% outpacing government +76% — AIP/Ontology platform is inflecting simultaneously in both markets.

shifts.note: Both segments accelerating simultaneously — platform pull-through across all customer types

shifts.trend: Operating income: FY25 $1.41B (4.5x YoY) → Q1 2026 $754M (already ~46% of FY25 total in one quarter); contribution margin 66%
shifts.note: Operating leverage compounding rapidly as fixed costs dilute into growing revenue

shifts.trend: Commercial growth outpacing government for the first time: commercial +95% vs gov +76%; AIP/Agents platform cited as the driver
shifts.note: Commercial inflection is the thesis unlock — government growth was always credible

shifts.trend: FY25: government 54% / commercial 46%; Q1 2026: government ~53% / commercial ~47% — mix slowly shifting commercial
shifts.note: Gradual mix shift toward higher-multiple commercial revenue

PLTR.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — the +85% growth acceleration is among the most dramatic in any software company at Palantir's scale. Commercial +95% is the key unlock that de-risks the government-dependency narrative.

PWR.headline: Revenue +26% YoY with backlog reaching $48.47B (+10% in a single quarter); data center infrastructure continues as primary growth catalyst.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $28.5B +20.3% → Q1 FY26 $7.87B +26.3%; Electric segment: FY25 $23.0B +21.0% → Q1 +$1.52B YoY
shifts.note: Acceleration in both revenue and operating income on higher margin Electric backlog
Backlog adding $4.5B in 90 days — customers pre-committing to multi-year projects

shifts.trend: Q1 FY26 operating margin 4.3% vs 3.8% prior year; gross margin 14.1% vs 13.4%; net income +55%
shifts.note: Margin expansion on Electric segment volume as data center work is higher-complexity/higher-margin

PWR.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — Quanta's backlog trajectory ($4.5B added in 90 days) gives 2-year revenue visibility; the data center electrical infrastructure wave is extending well into 2027-2028.

SNOW.headline: FY26 revenue +31% to $4.7B with RPO at $9.8B and $1M+ customers growing +27% to 733 — Snowflake stabilizing growth above 30% on AI Data Cloud demand.

shifts.trend: Product revenue: Q3 FY26 $1.16B +29% → FY26 ~$4.5B implied +31%; total revenue $4.7B vs $3.6B prior year
shifts.note: Growth stable at ~30% — not accelerating but not decelerating either

shifts.trend: RPO: Q3 $7.9B (48% next 12mo) → FY26 end $9.8B (46% next 12mo); 24% RPO growth in 5 months
shifts.note: RPO build signals forward demand expanding even if headline growth is stable

shifts.trend: Fortune 500 accounts: 785 FY26; $1M+ customers: Q3 FY26 growing → FY26 end 733 (+27% vs 576 prior year); broader enterprise diversification
shifts.note: Customer quality improving — enterprise deal sizes compounding

shifts.trend: Total customers 13,328 (+21% from 10,996); AI/data-cloud workloads cited as primary growth driver
shifts.note: Platform expansion beyond pure data warehouse as AI query workloads compound

SNOW.verdict: Thesis intact — Snowflake stabilizing at 30%+ growth with improving enterprise depth is the base case. The $9.8B RPO confirms demand; AI data platform positioning is the multi-year thesis for continued 25-35% growth.

SNPS.headline: Revenue +66% YoY in Q1 driven by Ansys ($886M); Design IP -6% organic is genuine weakness; backlog steady at $11.3B as Processor IP divested to GFS.

shifts.trend: Revenue: FY25 $7.05B +15% (Ansys contributed $756.6M) → Q1 FY26 $2.41B +66% (Ansys contributed $885.6M); organic excl-Ansys mixed
shifts.note: Headline growth inflated by Ansys; underlying EDA organic growth ~15%

shifts.trend: Design IP: FY25 weak (China -22% ex-Ansys) → Q1 FY26 $407M -6% YoY; Design Automation $2.0B +96% (Ansys-driven)
shifts.note: Design IP weakness is pre-dating Ansys and persisting — foundry customer softness + BIS impact

shifts.trend: $3.5B term loan paid off in Q1 FY26 (used for Ansys cash consideration); $13.5B FY25-end debt being deleveraged before buybacks resume
shifts.note: Aggressive debt paydown signals management prioritizing balance sheet restoration

shifts.trend: Share repurchase suspended post-Ansys close; $194.3M authorization remaining; Processor IP divested to GFS to reallocate resources
shifts.note: Capital return suspension is temporary — management committed to resuming buybacks post-delever

SNPS.verdict: Story complex — Ansys inflation makes organic growth hard to read; Design IP -6% is the concerning signal. Thesis depends on EDA demand durability as chip complexity drives spending regardless of Design IP softness.

VRT.headline: Revenue +30% with Americas +53% and gross margin expanding 400 bps YoY to 37.7% — Vertiv is demonstrating both revenue acceleration and margin expansion simultaneously.

shifts.note: Americas continues to accelerate above FY trend — hyperscaler liquid cooling orders pulling

shifts.trend: Gross margin: FY25 implied ~35%+ → Q1 FY26 37.7% (+400bps YoY); Americas operating margin 27.0% (vs 21.9% Q1 prior year)
shifts.note: 400bps YoY GM expansion confirms pricing power and scale benefits are compounding

shifts.trend: Combined order backlog: FY25 $15.0B (2x vs $7.2B prior year) → Q1 FY26 growing; 2-year delivery visibility on liquid cooling systems
shifts.note: Backlog more than doubled YoY — demand outpacing capacity by a wide margin

shifts.trend: FY26 capex guided $425-525M (incl. capitalized software) — investing in manufacturing capacity for liquid cooling/UPS
shifts.note: Capex investing through the backlog cycle; operating cash flow more than self-funds

VRT.verdict: Thesis strongly intact — Vertiv is at the center of the liquid cooling bottleneck; simultaneous revenue acceleration (+30%) and margin expansion (+400bps) is the ideal scenario. Backlog at $15B+ gives multi-year confidence.
